data.title: Bitcoin Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass indicates that if Bitcoin's price falls below $107,000, the cumulative liquidation intensity of long positions on major centralized exchanges (CEX) will reach $729 million. Conversely, if Bitcoin surpasses $110,000, the cumulative liquidation intensity of short positions on these exchanges will amount to $850 million.

BlockBeats notes that the liquidation chart does not display the exact number of contracts pending liquidation or their precise value. Instead, the chart's bars represent the relative importance of each liquidation cluster compared to nearby clusters, indicating intensity.

Thus, the chart illustrates the potential impact on the market when the asset price reaches certain levels. A higher 'liquidation bar' suggests a more intense reaction due to liquidity waves when the price hits that point.

data.title: RWA project Elevexai deployed to MANTRA Chain
data.description: MANTRA announced on the X platform that its Real Estate Investment Exchange (RWA) project, Elevexai, was officially deployed to the MANTRA Chain earlier this week. Elevexai is a joint RWAccelerator (April 2025 batch) incubation project launched by MANTRA and Google Cloud. The project focuses on tokenizing real estate assets, with its core product being the Real Estate Investment Exchange (REIX), which provides tokenized trading services for real estate projects and indices.

data.title: MERL briefly broke through 0.36 USDT, with the highest 24-hour increase reaching 31.6%.
data.description: OKX market data shows that MERL briefly broke through 0.36 USDT and is currently trading at 0.366 USDT, with the highest 24-hour increase reaching 31.6%.

data.title: An ancient address on the Ethereum network transferred 4,000 ETH to Binance, with a return on investment of 389 times.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, 14 hours ago, an ancient Ethereum address deposited 4,000 ETH (worth approximately $16 million) into Binance. These ETH were accumulated nine years ago at an average price of $10.25. If sold in full at the current price, the depositor would realize a profit of approximately $15.96 million, a 389x return on investment.

data.title: Hyperliquid has spent over $640 million on token buybacks so far this year, representing 2.1% of the total supply.
data.description: According to Coingecko analysis, Hyperliquid has set a record for the largest token buyback so far this year, spending over $644.64 million. This represents a total of at least 21.36 million HYPE tokens, representing 2.1% of the total supply. HYPE token buybacks have averaged $65.5 million per month, with a low of $39.14 million in March and a high of $110.62 million in August. The average buyback price for HYPE tokens has been approximately $30.18 so far.

data.title: Tether freezes 13.4 million USDT from 22 addresses on the Ethereum and Tron networks
data.description: According to MistTrack, Tether froze 13.4 million USDT across 22 addresses on the Ethereum and TRON chains on October 16. Among them, 10.3 million USDT were frozen at addresses beginning with 0xecb, and 1.4 million USDT were frozen at addresses beginning with TYzDe.

data.title: Crypto Market Faces Stagnation After Recent Plunge, Says Chris Burniske
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Chris Burniske, former head of crypto at Ark Invest and current partner at Placeholder VC, expressed concerns about the recent downturn in the crypto market. He noted that last Friday's sharp decline has led to a short-term stagnation, making it difficult for sustained buying to occur quickly after such a crash. Burniske highlighted that this cycle has been disappointing for many, potentially limiting actions as investors await a market recovery or previous historical highs.

Burniske advised against getting caught up in minor chart details, suggesting that the monthly charts for Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) indicate that the market remains in a high range, albeit with some cracks. He mentioned that if considering profit-taking, it is essential to keep this broader perspective in mind.

He observed that MicroStrategy's stock is declining, gold is issuing warnings, and the credit market is also signaling caution, with stocks likely to react last. While a weak rebound is always possible, Burniske has already taken action, reminding investors that cashing out is never an all-or-nothing decision.

Burniske plans to monitor Bitcoin's response to the $100,000 mark but expressed potential interest in the market again if Bitcoin falls to $75,000 or lower. He concluded by stating that this bull market is different from previous ones, and the next bear market will also be distinct.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $536 million yesterday, with none of the twelve ETFs experiencing a net inflow.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $536 million yesterday (October 16th, EST). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow yesterday was the Ark Invest and 21Shares ETF (ARKB), which saw a net outflow of $275 million. ARKB's total net inflow has now reached $2.057 billion. The second largest daily outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FBTC), which saw a net outflow of $132 million. FBTC's total net inflow has now reached $12.612 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $146.435 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.81%, and cumulative net inflows reaching $61.905 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $56.8766 million yesterday, with only BlackRock ETHA seeing a net inflow
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $56.8766 million yesterday (October 16th, EST). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow yesterday was the Blackrock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $46.9042 million, bringing ETHA's total net inflow to $14.389 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow yesterday was the Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a net outflow of $69.0344 million, bringing ETHE's total net outflow to $4.651 billion. As of press time, the Ethereum spot ETF's total net asset value was $26.508 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.7%, and cumulative net inflows reaching $14.829 billion.

data.title: This week's mining data highlights: Cango terminates its ADR program, US institutional investors undergo structural upgrades, and the network's hash rate increases by 8.39%.
data.description: Week 42, 2025 (October 10-16): 1. According to cloverpool, the average Bitcoin network hashrate was 1,080 EH/s, with a peak of 1,140 EH/s and a low of 990 EH/s, an increase of 8.39% from the previous week's average hashrate (996 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average Bitcoin price was $114,838, with a peak of $122,600 and a low of $107,000, a decrease of 6.35% from the previous week's average price ($126,296). 3. Mining news worth noting include: (1) Cango: Will terminate its American Depositary Receipt (ADR) program and plan to list directly on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in the form of Class A common stock; (2) Canaan Inc.: Mining output in September was 92 BTC, and its Bitcoin holdings increased to 1,582; (3) Nasdaq-listed Tianci signed an MOU with BTC Digital to purchase crypto mining machines for US$5 million. Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company.

data.title: Analyst: When the gold market cools down, capital will turn to Bitcoin, which is known as digital gold
data.description: On Thursday, as gold prices climbed to a new all-time high of $4,357 per ounce, its market capitalization hit a record $30 trillion. This means that gold's market capitalization is now 14.5 times that of Bitcoin, which is approximately $2.1 trillion. It is also 1.5 times higher than the combined market capitalization of the world's "Big Seven" technology companies—Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Tesla—which is approximately $20 trillion. Investors have flocked to gold due to a devaluation of the US dollar, geopolitical tensions, and trade tariffs, and its price has soared 64% since January 1st. Many analysts believe that when the gold market cools, capital will shift to Bitcoin, known as digital gold. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: QMMM Holdings Faces SEC Investigation Amid Stock Manipulation Allegations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, U.S. Nasdaq-listed company QMMM Holdings (NASDAQ:QMMM) announced plans on September 9, 2025, to establish a $100 million cryptocurrency reserve, leading to a 9.6-fold increase in its stock price over three weeks. However, by the end of September, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) suspected the company of using social media platforms to manipulate its stock price, resulting in a suspension of trading from September 29. The suspension remains in effect.

A visit to QMMM's Hong Kong headquarters on October 16 revealed that the office was deserted. Nearby company employees indicated that QMMM had relocated in September, but the new location remains unknown. Reports state that QMMM was listed on Nasdaq in July 2024, with its registration in the Cayman Islands as a holding company. It operates two subsidiaries, Manymany Creations and Quantum Matrix, which focus on media and animation production. Both are registered near the Tin Hau MTR station in Hong Kong, with business types listed as "advertising and market research."

data.title: Binance: ZEROBASE (ZBT) token will be available for trading today at 7:00 PM
data.description: According to official announcements, the ZEROBASE (ZBT) token circulation will begin at 7:00 PM (UTC+8) on October 17, 2025. Users who participated in the ZEROBASE Pre-TGE can trade ZBT tokens on Binance Alpha. Winners of the first Booster Fundraising event can claim and trade their ZBT tokens within eight hours of the token circulation launch.

data.title: Founder of Bridgewater Associates: Stablecoins are not a good way to store wealth, and has held a small amount of BTC for many years
data.description: In a recent interview with Caixin, Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, analyzed the characteristics of Bitcoin and stablecoins, as well as their roles in investment portfolios. He stated that he has held a small portion of Bitcoin for many years, and his investment proportion has remained unchanged. He sees Bitcoin as a diversification asset relative to gold. However, Bitcoin also has its drawbacks, and central banks are reluctant to hold it. He added that stablecoins are not a good storehold of wealth. Their essence is that they can only be exchanged for a corresponding currency and do not generate interest. Therefore, from a financial perspective, holding stablecoins is not as good as holding interest-bearing fiat assets. The advantage of stablecoins is that they are globally accepted and act as a clearinghouse system for convenient transactions, making them suitable for those who do not care about interest. As for whether stablecoins can solve the problem of US Treasury bonds, he believes that if stablecoin buyers already hold US Treasury bonds, it is equivalent to transferring US Treasury bonds from one pocket to another. Whether this will increase demand for US Treasury bonds remains to be seen.

data.title: Ethereum has attracted over 16,000 new developers to join its ecosystem in the first three quarters of this year
data.description: According to data from Electric Capital cited by the Ethereum Foundation, over 16,000 new developers joined the Ethereum ecosystem between January and September of this year. Solana was the second most popular destination for new developers, with over 11,500 joining the ecosystem; however, a representative from the Solana Foundation stated that these figures may be outdated. Meanwhile, Bitcoin added nearly 7,500 new developers. The Ethereum ecosystem has the largest active developer community of any blockchain project, with 31,869 developers. In comparison, Solana ranks second with 17,708 developers, and Bitcoin has 11,036 developers. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: TeraWulf subsidiary completes pricing of $3.2 billion in senior secured notes to fund New York data center expansion
data.description: TeraWulf (NASDAQ: WULF) announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, WULF Compute LLC, has priced $3.2 billion in 7.75% senior secured notes due 2030, with expected closing on October 23, 2025. The proceeds will be used to fund a data center expansion project at TeraWulf's Lake Mariner, New York, campus. The notes are guaranteed by several subsidiaries and secured by pledges of related assets and equity. Morgan Stanley served as sole bookrunner.

data.description:
Florida House Rep. Webster Barnaby filed a new crypto reserve bill after the first one failed, but this time it isn’t a Bitcoin-only bill, and stricter compliance measures have been added.

data.title: Florida Proposes Bill to Invest in Digital Assets
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a revised bill has been introduced in the Florida House to permit the state to invest in digital assets, including Bitcoin and crypto ETFs. This follows the withdrawal of an earlier proposal by the operations subcommittee in June. The new legislation, known as Florida House Bill 183, was presented by lawmaker Webster Barnaby. It aims to allow the state and certain public entities to allocate up to 10% of their funds into digital assets such as Bitcoin, crypto exchange-traded products, crypto securities, non-fungible tokens, and other blockchain-based products. This bill builds upon Barnaby's previous attempt, HB 487, which was dismissed in June. The updated proposal introduces new standards for custody, documentation, and fiduciary responsibilities related to holding and lending digital assets. Additionally, it broadens the scope of investible digital assets beyond Bitcoin, offering Florida more flexibility in diversifying its digital asset portfolio if the bill is enacted.

HB 183 is set to take effect on July 1, 2026, and would authorize the State Board of Administration to invest pension and other trust funds in digital assets. During the 2025 legislative session, numerous Bitcoin and digital asset reserve bills were proposed across various state legislatures, but only three were successfully enacted into law. These include bills from Arizona, New Hampshire, and Texas. New Hampshire's HB 302 permits the treasurer to invest up to 5% of public funds in digital assets with market caps exceeding $500 billion, currently limited to Bitcoin. Texas Senate Bill 21 establishes a Bitcoin-only reserve, while Arizona's HB 2749 allows for the creation of a digital asset reserve from unclaimed property.

In addition to HB 183, Barnaby has introduced another bill, HB 175, aimed at easing regulatory requirements for stablecoin issuers in Florida. This bill seeks to clarify that recognized payment stablecoin issuers should not be required to obtain separate licenses or registrations. It mandates that stablecoin issuers be fully collateralized with US dollars or treasuries and conduct a public audit of their reserves at least once a month. Like HB 183, the stablecoin bill is also intended to take effect on July 1, 2026.

In related news, California Governor Gavin Newsom recently signed a law to protect unclaimed crypto from being automatically sold. The California SB 822 ensures that digital assets are preserved in their original form rather than being converted to cash before being transferred to state custody. This law allows crypto account holders to recover their original assets by filing a valid claim with the California State Controller.
